project: iClaw Investment Experts and Memory Performance Refactor
description: Refactor 智能投资专家 and 记忆管理 so that list/content views load from lightweight snapshots while heavy detail and runtime diagnostics move off the first-paint path.

userStories.title: Add a lightweight investment experts catalog endpoint
userStories.description: As a desktop client, I want a lightweight 投资专家列表接口 so that the page can load card data without downloading the full agent catalog payload.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a dedicated control-plane endpoint for investment experts list data instead of reusing the full /agents/catalog payload
The list response includes only card fields needed for the investment experts page
The list response excludes large metadata fields such as long system prompts and full previews
Installed state is not duplicated as heavyweight per-item metadata in the catalog response
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Keep the DTO intentionally narrow and projection-oriented.

userStories.title: Switch the desktop investment experts page to snapshot-first loading
userStories.description: As a user, I want 智能投资专家 to open immediately from cached list data so that revisiting the page does not trigger a cold full refresh every time.
acceptanceCriteria: InvestmentExpertsView no longer forces a full refresh on initial mount
Desktop investment experts loading uses cached snapshot data first when available
A background refresh updates the page without blocking first paint
Manual refresh still triggers a network refresh when requested by the user
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Preserve existing page behavior while removing mount-time forceRefresh.

userStories.title: Load investment expert detail data on demand
userStories.description: As a user, I want detailed expert metadata to load only when I open a card so that the list page stays light while detail remains rich.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a dedicated investment expert detail fetch path keyed by slug
Investment expert detail dialog loads full detail data only after the user opens a specific expert
The list page does not require full detail payloads to render card shelves
Closing and reopening the list page does not re-download full detail payloads for every expert
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This story should keep the current visual richness of the dialog without burdening the list payload.

userStories.title: Split memory entries loading from memory runtime diagnostics
userStories.description: As a user, I want 记忆管理 to render remembered content before any runtime health checks so that the page opens like a content workspace rather than a diagnostic console.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a fast memory snapshot path that returns entries and directories without spawning the runtime memory status CLI
MemoryView first paint depends on the fast entries snapshot instead of synchronous runtime diagnostics
Opening the memory page no longer blocks on memory status availability
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Keep the existing entry parsing behavior for now; this story is about cutting diagnostics out of the first-paint path.

userStories.title: Persist cached memory runtime status with TTL
userStories.description: As a desktop shell, I want a cached memory runtime status snapshot so that repeated page opens do not rerun the same expensive diagnostics within a short time window.
acceptanceCriteria: Introduce a persisted memory runtime status snapshot with saved timestamp
Memory runtime status reads reuse the cached snapshot for 30 to 60 seconds when still fresh
Expired or missing snapshots trigger a refresh path that updates the stored snapshot
Cache read and write behavior is scoped per desktop app brand/runtime workspace
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Prefer a simple local snapshot file or lightweight persisted store over repeated ad hoc runtime probes.

userStories.title: Load memory runtime status asynchronously after content render
userStories.description: As a user, I want memory diagnostics to appear after the list is already visible so that status chips never block access to my stored memories.
acceptanceCriteria: MemoryView renders entries before runtime status resolves
Runtime status is loaded in a secondary asynchronous path after the initial memory content render
Runtime status failure shows a non-blocking warning state instead of replacing or delaying the memory list
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This story is UI orchestration only; the cached status source should already exist from earlier stories.

userStories.title: Add explicit refresh controls for memory diagnostics
userStories.description: As a user, I want a manual way to re-check memory diagnostics so that heavyweight runtime inspection happens only when I ask for fresh status.
acceptanceCriteria: Memory diagnostics UI exposes a user-triggered refresh action for runtime status
Manual refresh bypasses the short TTL cache and requests a fresh runtime status probe
The refresh action indicates loading state without blocking memory list interactions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Do not reroute this action through the main memory entries load path.
